structure.format: MCQ in linked sets of 2-5 tied to a primary/secondary source (text, image, map, chart)
Short-Answer Questions, parts A/B/C. Q1+Q2 mandatory; choose Q3 OR Q4. ~13 min per SAQ.
Document-Based Question, 7 documents. 15 min reading + 45 min writing. Map may be provided as reference (NOT counted as document).
Long Essay Question. Choose 1 of 3 options across different period windows.

exam.geographic_coverage: Global — must avoid Europe-centric framing; CB explicitly emphasizes Africa, Asia, Americas, and global comparative perspective

historical_reasoning_skills: Sourcing — POV, Purpose, Historical Situation, Audience
vocabulary_cues: Identify one claim that the author makes
Describe one economic/political/social change in [region] that occurred as a result of
Explain one reason why [group] [verb] as suggested by the author
Identify one technological/military/economic factor that contributed to
Identify one likely audience for the claims
Explain how one ideology or set of ideas likely influenced
Explain one way that [group] used X to do Y
Evaluate the extent to which X affected Y during the period circa [year] to [year]
Develop an argument that evaluates the extent to which
saq_format_template: A. Identify/Describe one X. B. Describe/Explain one Y. C. Explain how/why Z.
sample_stems_frq: SAQ secondary source: Jack Weatherford (1988) on Africa & discovery of America — A. claim, B. economic change in Americas, C. why American Indians victims
SAQ primary source: Louise Otto-Peters 1849 German women's newspaper — A. audience, B. 19th c context, C. ideology
SAQ no stimulus: Muslim empires 1300-1600 (Ottoman/Safavid/Mughal) — A. tech/military factor, B. economic policy, C. tolerance reason
SAQ no stimulus: Revolutions 1750-1900 — A. factor causing outbreak, B. ideology used, C. challenge faced

DBQ: Evaluate the extent to which new transportation and/or communication technologies affected African societies during the period circa 1850 to 1960. (7 docs: Sierra Leone hut tax letter, S. Africa mining photo, Gold Coast telegrams, P&O Egypt brochure, TB report S. Africa, Gold Coast railway history, Toyin Falola Nigeria memoir)
LEQ 3: Economic rivalries primary motivation for European imperial expansion 1450-1750
